revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:アメノオシヒ]][[zh:天忍日命]]
{{Infobox deity | name = Amenooishi no Mikoto | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | kami_type = [[Amatsukami|Heavenly kami]] | full_name = Amenooishi no Mikoto | other_names = Amenooshihi no Mikoto, Kamisahi no Mikoto | aliases = | divine_Rank = | role = God of war | burial_place = | parents =Father: {{ill|Amenoiwakumonobe Yasukunotama no Mikoto|ja|天石門別安国玉主命|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoiwakumonobe Yasukunotama no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天石門別神}} or {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}}| siblings = | spouse = | children = {{ill|Amenowoshito no Mikoto|ja|天押人命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenowoshito no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | palace = | shrines = [[Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine]], [[Tomobayashi Uji Shrine]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, {{ill|Maruko clan|ja|丸子氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruko clan|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368098}}, {{ill|Saeki Clan|ja|佐伯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Hibametsukai clan|ja|日奉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibametsukai clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西党|1=Hihafuri clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11627255}} | relatives = {{ill|Michinoomi-no-mikoto|qid=Q11641798|1=Michinoomi}}; {{ill|Q110418588|qid=Q110418588|1=Daughter of Tomo clan, wife of Minamoto no Munekai}} }} '''Amenooshihi''' (天忍日命) is a deity in [[Japanese mythology]], mentioned in sources such as the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. The name is also written as "Amenooshihi no Mikoto" (天押日命) or "Kamisahi no Mikoto" (神狭日命) in various texts. Amenooshihi is the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan (ancient)|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} (Ōtomo no Muraji/Ōtomo no Sukune). He is said to have accompanied {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|邇邇芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandchild|1=Tenson kōrin}}. == Genealogy == The genealogical details of Amenooshihi are not recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. In the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}} and {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} ("{{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Jindai Hongi|ja|神代本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jindai Hongi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀}}"), Amenooshihi is described as a child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. The "Jindai Hongi" also refers to him as Kamisahi no Mikoto. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists Amenooshihi as a fifth- or sixth-generation descendant of Takamimusubi. In the genealogies of the Ōtomo clan, he is described as the son of Takamimusubi's fourth-generation descendant, {{ill|Amenoiwakumonobe Yasukunotama no Mikoto|ja|天石門別安国玉主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoiwakumonobe Yasukunotama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天石門別神|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940542}}. == Recorded Accounts == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandchild|1=Tenson kōrin}} of Ninigi, Amenooshihi and {{ill|Amanokume no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanokume no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}) acted as escorts. They carried strong quivers on their backs, wore swords at their waists, and held bows and arrows made of heavenly materials.<ref name="kojiki">''Kojiki,'' Shogakukan, 2004, p. 117 (Japan Knowledge Edition).</ref> In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} (Jindai section, Chapter 9, Alternate Account 4), Amenooshihi led the heavenly warriors, carrying a strong quiver, wearing a divine tsubo-armor, and holding bows and arrows, including the {{ill|Hachime Kabura|ja|八目鳴鏑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachime Kabura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, to clear the way for Ninigi.<ref name="nihonshoki">''Nihon Shoki,'' Shogakukan, 2002, pp. 144-145 (Japan Knowledge Edition).</ref> Similar traditions are found in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}, the "Jindai Hongi" and "Tenjin Hongi" sections of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, and the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}. == Descendant Clans == Both the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} recognize Amenooshihi as the ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}. The {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} ("Jindai Hongi" and "Tenjin Hongi") and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}} also identify him as the ancestor of the Ōtomo no Sukune. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} lists several clans descended from Amenooshihi: * Left Capital Divine Lineage: Ōtomo no Sukune - Descendants of Amenooshihi no Mikoto, fifth-generation descendant of Takamimusubi. * Right Capital Divine Lineage: Saeki Hibametsukai no Miyatsuko - Descendants of Amenooshihi’s eleventh-generation descendant {{ill|Ōtomo Muroya|ja|大伴室屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Muroya|qq=}}. * Yamato Divine Lineage: Takashi no Muraji - Descendants of Amenooshihi’s eleventh-generation descendant Ōtomo Muroya. * Kawachi Divine Lineage: Saeki no Ōtoshi - Descendants of Amenooshihi’s eleventh-generation descendant Ōtomo Muroya. The differing kabane (titles) in these records reflect the change in kabane during the enactment of the {{ill|Eight Rank System|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} in 684 AD.<ref>{{Harvid|Ōtomo Clan (Ancient Lineage)|2010|qq=}}</ref> == Analysis == The name "Oshihi" is interpreted as "Great Spirit" or "Heroic Spirit."<ref name="kojiki"/> The traditions surrounding Amenooshihi, as well as related figures such as {{ill|Michinoomi no Mikoto|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}}, illustrate the historical role of the Ōtomo clan as guardians of the emperor and suppressors of unrest.<ref>{{Harvid|Ōtomo Clan (Ancient Lineage)|2010|qq=}}</ref> == Worship == Amenooshihi is enshrined at the following locations: * {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujidera|1=Fujiidera}}) * Kōban Shrine ({{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kanan, Osaka}}) * Aburahi Shrine ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōka, Shiga|en|Kōka, Shiga|qid=Q847584|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka, Shiga}}) * Yabusa Shrine ({{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|qid=Q861519|lt=Iki|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}) * {{ill|Shitomi Shrine|ja|志等美神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitomi Shrine|qq=}} Upper Shrine ({{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}) - Enshrined as "Ame no Oshiamano Mikoto." * {{ill|Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|ja|大伴神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Shrine (Saku)|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|qid=Q495821|lt=Saku|1=Saku, Nagano}}) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandchild|1=Tenson kōrin}} {{Shinto|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|アメノオシヒ|version=100745888|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:アメノオシヒ]]
<!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--><!--Ame-no-Oshihi--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:天忍日命]] {{wikidata link|Q10939616}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan (ancient Japan)]] [[Category:Amatsukami]]
